reasoning:
Based on comprehensive evidence up to Oct 26, 2025:

Evidence AGAINST ground incursion:
- Historical base rate: Zero US ground incursions into Iran in 45+ years despite ongoing tensions
- US actively withdrawing forces from Iraq (by Sept 2025 per agreement), decreasing regional footprint not increasing
- Diplomatic track active: Trump team proposed talks in June, reducing strike probability from 67% to 46%
- Polymarket gave only 15% probability for US forces ever entering Iran in 2025 (June data)
- UN Security Council actively engaged, E3 offered offramp: extend Oct 2025 deadline if Iran cooperates
- High costs and risks of ground invasion versus limited objectives (nuclear program can be targeted via air)
- US intelligence assessed Iran not building nuclear weapon as of March 2025
- Special forces/advisors excluded from resolution criteria, further narrowing qualifying scenarios

Evidence FOR ground incursion:
- October 2025 snapback deadline creates potential flashpoint
- Trump has threatened airstrikes if no deal reached
- Iran threatened NPT withdrawal if sanctions snapback triggered
- Regional tensions remain high with Iranian-backed militia threats

The probability of actual ground invasion is extremely low. Even airstrike probability (Polymarket) only reached ~50% at peaks and fell during diplomatic openings. Ground incursion is significantly more escalatory than airstrikes and carries much higher political/military costs. The 8% probability reflects the small but non-zero chance that complete diplomatic collapse combined with Iranian nuclear breakout could lead to unforeseen escalation chain resulting in limited ground incursion, though this scenario remains highly improbable given all available evidence.
